rows.notes: Very clear centered year at top of page. Ink and digits legible, no amounts in columns.
Page title immediately beneath the year. Handwriting clear; treated as a header (no amounts).

rows.description: Epton glade Rector. Instalment of former year undertaker
rows.notes: Pounds, shillings and pence columns clearly show 3 | 2 | 6. Description partly ambiguous in the middle words (handwritten abbreviation), but the name and nature ('Rector. Instalment') are legible.

rows.description: Rolls. Kidlington Int' a draining; money to Michr 1854
rows.notes: Pounds (9) and shillings (12) align with the two leftmost amount columns; there is no clear pence figure in the rightmost column. Description is fairly legible though some abbreviations (Int') and 'Michr' are interpreted from context.
Amount shows 1 in pounds column and 13 in shillings column; no pence visible. Description 'Hewett. Wittenham Int on Do' is readable though abbreviated.
All three amount columns clearly read 4 | 13 | 4. Short description with repeated 'Do' is legible.

rows.description: Merton Fire. repayment to Domus &t Michr 1854
rows.notes: Amounts clearly aligned as 23 | 10 | 10. 'Merton Fire' and 'repayment to Domus' are legible; date 1854 visible.

rows.description: Do Do to Do (to Lady Day) 1855
rows.notes: Amounts identical to previous line: 23 | 10 | 10 and clearly aligned. Description uses 'Do' abbreviations; 'Lady Day 1855' readable.
Only a single number '20' appears in the pounds column; shillings and pence columns are blank. Description 'Clutterbuck 2nd and 3rd Instalments' readable though slightly cramped.

rows.description: Expenses in purchase of Little (Henry charged) in Mr. Walsh File 1853 how charged on the living fund
rows.notes: Amounts read 7 | 15 | 10 and align well with the three vertical rulings. Description long but individual words (Little, Henry, Walsh, 1853) are legible; some parentheses and phrasing are inferred from handwriting.
Three amounts clearly read 25 | 16 | 6. Name 'Keeley' and 'Rent to Mich near 1854' interpretation consistent with surrounding entries.
Amounts read 25 | 5 | 4. 'Do - to Lady Day 1855' is clearly the follow-up to the previous 'Keeley' entry (abbreviation 'Do').
Two numbers visible as '20' and '4'; alignment suggests pounds = 20 and shillings = 4, with no pence figure visible. 'Michmas' (Michaelmas) inferred from abbreviation.
Three columns read clearly as 22 | 18 | 5. 'Do' indicates continuation from previous Freeman entry; 'Lady Day 1855' visible.
Amounts align as 6 | 19 | 2. The trust name and 'Div. Consols' are legible; '14' appears to be part of the description (possibly a folio or reference).
Large pound figure clearly written as 370 in the pounds column and 6 | 6 in shillings/pence. The proper name 'Tanshaw' and phrase 'Banner Inc tum Domus' are somewhat uncertain but readable.
Large clear amount 690 | 18 | 5; the plus sign and repetition of 'do' indicate continuation/another item of the same type. Handwriting of amounts extremely clear.
Single clear number '50' in the pounds column; shillings and pence blank. Description 'Dean. Fees.' is legible but brief.

rows.description: Lightfoot Instalment amount of loan & repayment
rows.notes: A single number '40' is visible in pounds column. 'Lightfoot' and 'Instalment' legible; remainder of description inferred from cramped handwriting.
Single '1' in pounds column aligned with this short description. 'Colby - Keys' clearly readable.

rows.description: Div. Consols or Sums transferred to Colby by late Rector &c to be of Wm Jones recd late Aprill 1855
rows.notes: Only a '14' is visible in the pounds column. The description has many abbreviations and small script; interpretation of 'Div. Consols or Sums transferred to Colby' is best fit to the handwriting but some words (names and dates) are faint.

rows.description: Repayment to Domus from Library acct for work done in preparing a plan for Books during the rebuilding of the Library
rows.notes: Amounts clearly read 42 | 12 | 6 and align with vertical rules. The long description is legible though written in flowing script; interpretation of some words (e.g., 'rebuilding') confirmed by context.

rows.description: Mistake in last years acct the sum having been charged for drawing on forfeiting etc (see note)
rows.notes: Two rightmost amount columns show '38' and '10' aligned but the pence column appears blank — alignment suggests pounds = 38, shillings = 10. The descriptive paragraph is cramped and several words are difficult; 'Mistake in last years acct' is clear.

rows.description: By late Rector Ex Con (note regarding Gosling Bank cheque for Mr. Sanders Div.)
rows.notes: This short interline note is present beneath the 'Mistake' paragraph; it contains no aligned monetary figures. The text is small and partially crowded but the general sense (reference to late Rector and Gosling Bank cheque) is discernible.
Single clear '26' in pounds column. 'Legmate fine due 1850' is legible though 'Legmate' could be alternative spelling; context suggests a legacy/legatee fine.
Two numbers appear as '4' and '5', aligned in pounds and shillings columns. The name 'Lisler' and 'Survidge' are uncertain readings of a cramped name; transcription chosen as closest visual match.

rows.description: + from Building Fund (for sewer & new Building) Breach? (note)
rows.notes: Right-hand stacked numbers are congested and partly overwritten. I read the leftmost group as '187' in pounds, '6' shillings, '12' pence, and a trailing small loop that resembles an 'ob' (halfpenny) — however pence = 12 would violate the 0-11 rule; therefore interpretation is uncertain. Alternative readings could be 187 | 12 | 5/12; chosen values reflect best alignment reading but confidence is low. (Marked as low confidence because column boundaries are ambiguous in this cluster.)

rows.description: The sums and notes at foot (various smaller adjustments and carried figures)
rows.notes: Lower-left cluster of small printed totals and marginal notes is very cramped. I read one right-hand figure as '165 0 6' (pounds|shillings|pence) but the handwriting is faint and there are several overstruck digits and marginal annotations. Treated as a total/adjustment line; confidence low.

Bottom-right large number reads visually as '361 4' (pounds and shillings) with the pence either blank or not shown. This looks like the page final total. Handwriting slightly smudged; pence unclear so left blank.
